---
title: Daniel Chen
toc: false
---

::: {.columns}

::: {.column}

![](img/Daniel%20Chen.png){width="90%"}

:::

::: {.column}

Hi I'm Daniel. I'm a Lecturer at the University of British Columbia.
I primary teach Data Science tooling through the Statistics department,
and through the Master's of Data Science program at UBC.

Previously, I was a data science educator and developer advocate at [Posit, PBC](https://posit.co/). They are the authors of a _TON_ of [open source data science tools](https://opensource.posit.co/), some of which are used in this tutorial. They'll be in the PyCon US 2026 Expo hall (booth 539),
so check them out!

:::

:::

## How it's made

If you're curious about the tools about how this tutorial site was made:

- Quarto Website + Quarto Slides
    - <https://quarto.org/>
- Shinylive = Shiny + WebAssembly
    - <https://shiny.posit.co/py/get-started/shinylive.html>
    - Shinylive + quarto:
        - <https://github.com/quarto-ext/shinylive>
        - <https://quarto-ext.github.io/shinylive/>
- `brand.yml`: Specifying colors and branding
    - <https://posit-dev.github.io/brand-yml/>
- Claude Code skills
    - All of the branding setup was done though a Claude Code skill. I just pointed Claude to the PyCon website and Google Sheets template, and asked it to make me a `_brand.yml` file.
        - <https://github.com/posit-dev/skills>
